channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Government creates fund to help diversify economy
item.description:
Initially, the Government Guidance Fund will be worth 11 billion patacas, but its size could reach 20 billion patacas, indicated the Secretary for Economy and Finance. This fund aims to promote the “strengthening of economic resilience”, through the diversification of the territory’s economy, creating “more quality jobs” and reinforcing its diversification. It also constitutes a “relevant instrument” to deepen Macau-Hengqin articulation and to promote the coordinated development of the Greater Bay Area, with the hope that it will attract “quality” qualified companies and staff.
Catarina Pereira
The Government will create a fund of 11 billion patacas, coming from the income accumulated over the years from the financial reserve, to promote the “strengthening of economic resilience”. The idea is to promote, “in a solid way, the adequate diversification of the economy and the push for industrial modernization”, explained the Secretary for Economy and Finance. Other objectives include supporting the transformation of scientific and technological results and their industrialization, “creating more quality jobs” and “strengthening the diversification of jobs” in the territory. Tai Kin Ip said that the Government Guidance Fund “aims, through the financial participation of the Government, to guide different types of private capital for investments that focus on the main emerging industries and industries of Macau's future”. The fund is expected to reach MOP20 billion. “The aim is, in this way, to encourage, bring together and guide the mobilization of these private capitals, attracting quality companies and qualified fund managers to participate in investments in priority sectors guided by the Government”, added the government official. The Executive will “clearly” define the priority sectors and development guidelines and, stated Tai Kin Ip, “by establishing mechanisms and rules for the selection of qualified fund managers”, it will create conditions for “proactive market participation, taking full advantage of the professionalism, flexibility and high efficiency of market functioning”. The Secretary also highlighted that the fund also constitutes a “relevant instrument” to deepen the Macau-Hengqin articulation and to promote the coordinated development of the Greater Bay Area. “The Fund will help to attract quality companies, qualified scientific and technological research staff and the financial sector to settle and concentrate in Macau, Hengqin and the Greater Bay Area”, he announced. The advisor to the Office of the Secretary for Economy and Finance, Tam Chi Neng, indicated, in turn, that the Fund will be supervised by the Chief Executive and will have a government management entity specifically responsible for day-to-day management. “The selection of qualified fund managers will be carried out through market-oriented mechanisms, with rigorous selection criteria”, he added. A Guidance Council for the fund will also be set up, made up of Government leaders, professionals, academics and representatives from relevant areas, “with the competence to issue opinions regarding policy guidelines, strategic planning and matters of special relevance to the Fund”, clarified Tam Chi Neng.
“Longtermism” and “patient capital” The advisor explained that the fund will pursue the principles of “long termism” and “patient capital”, prioritizing the promotion of emerging industries and industries of the future with an effective impact on boosting the economy. According to Tam Chi Neng at the fund's presentation conference, the priority areas include national strategy, industrial modernization, scientific and technological innovation and ensuring the well-being of the population, but also "domains aligned with the positioning of 'One Center, One Platform and One Base', which allow the maximization of Macau's distinctive advantages; investment projects that support coordinated development between the MSAR, the Cooperation Zone and the Greater Bay Area; and other investment projects aligned with the policy guidelines defined by the Macao SAR Government”. Tam Chi Neng indicated that the Government will complete, during this year, the creation of the fund, and then begin the selection procedure for fund managers. In parallel, work will be promoted to establish the management entity and promulgate administrative regulations and complementary diplomas that frame the functioning of the Government Guidance Fund. Communication and consultation mechanisms will also begin with private capital investors in the first phase, “with the expectation of guiding them in their reaction and participation in the Macao SAR Government's priority industrial investments”, stated the advisor.

item.title: The game's recovery slowed down in February
item.description:
For the fifth consecutive month, Macau's casinos surpassed the 20 billion patacas threshold in gross revenues, but February's results left mixed signals, with annual growth of 4.5% contrasting with a monthly drop of 8.9%, after turnover fell short of projections at the beginning of Golden Week of the Chinese New Year. Official data also points to a slowdown in the rate of recovery compared to pre-pandemic levels
SERGIO TERRA
Gross gaming revenue in Macau amounted to around 20.63 billion patacas in February, a value that represents, on the one hand, an increase of 4.5% compared to the 19.74 billion recorded in the same period of 2025 and, on the other, a decrease of 8.9% compared to the previous month (22.63 billion), according to data published on the website of the Inspection and Coordination Department of Games (DICJ). Casinos exceeded 20 billion for the fifth consecutive month, however, they did not withdraw the dividends initially expected from the impact of the Golden Week of the Chinese New Year, celebrated between the 15th and 23rd, despite the daily average of February (736.68 million) having slightly exceeded that of January (730.10 million), a month in which gross revenues had grown 24% in annual terms, reaching 90.7% of the level of the same period of 2019. Last month's results show a slowdown in the pace of post-pandemic recovery, corresponding to 81.3% of the amount recorded in February 2019, falling short of the recovery seen in the fourth quarter of 2025 (91.5%) and in the overall year (84.6%). According to the DICJ, the game's gross revenue increased by 13.9% to 43.26 billion patacas in the first two months of this year, compared to the 38 billion recorded in the same period of 2025. The amount collected in January and February is equivalent to 86% of the amount accumulated in the first two months of 2019 (50.31 million). Last week, investment bank JP Morgan decided to revise its forecasts for February downwards, noting that “holidays started slowly, with the first four to five days recording a daily rate of around 450 million patacas”, reflecting a double-digit annual drop. However, “from the sixth day onwards, activity [in casinos] increased sharply, with gross gaming revenue exceeding 1.2 billion patacas per day, which implies an increase of 10% to 15% compared to the previous year”, indicated DS Kim, Selina Li and Lindsey Qian, analysts at JP Morgan. According to data released by the Tourism Services Bureau, based on statistics from the Public Security Police Corps, Macau welcomed more than 1.55 million visitors during the Golden Week of the Year of the Horse, which represents a daily average of 172,699 and a year-on-year increase of 5.5%. It is worth remembering that the casinos closed their accounts for 2025 with total gross revenues of 247.40 billion patacas, 9.1% more than in the previous year. In the Macao SAR Budget for 2026, the Government predicted revenues of 236 billion patacas for this year's general calculation.
Galaxy attentive to the Japanese market Galaxy Entertainment may re-evaluate the possibility of investing in a casino-resort abroad, if a “suitable project” emerges, admitted its president, Francis Lui, at a press conference following the release of the financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025. According to the GGRAsia portal, when responding to questions from journalists, Lui did not commit to a specific external jurisdiction, but acknowledged that Japan – which is expected to launch a new round of applications for local governments in 2027 – would be a “really good market” to invest in. "We are very interested, but we need to understand the situation better before we make a decision. However, in general, I think Japan itself is a very attractive proposition," he declared. On the other hand, he was less enthusiastic about a possible bet on Thailand. “Thailand has been a good tourist country for many years, but with the Bhumjaithai Party now in a leading position in the coalition government, I believe it will take a few years for negotiations [on casino resorts] to return to the table,” he predicted.

item.title: Four more operators extend paid maternity leave
item.description:
Wynn, Melco, Galaxy and MGM decided to increase paid maternity leave, from 70 to 90 days, for workers with at least one year of service, following the measures announced by Sands last week. In addition, Melco, Galaxy and MGM also extended paternity leaves from five to seven days. The measures have been in force since yesterday, with SJM being the only gaming operator that has not yet announced the extension of its licenses
Following Sands' announcement last week, four more gaming dealerships announced the extension of maternity leaves for their employees, according to statements issued by the companies. Wynn, Melco, Galaxy and MGM decided to extend paid leave from 70 to 90 days for mothers of newborns, with the last three also increasing paternity leave from five to seven days. The measures came into force yesterday. Among gaming concessionaires, all that remains is for SJM to update its paid leave policies for parents of newborns. In a statement, Wynn said the new initiative reflects “the company’s strong commitment to the well-being of its workers and family-friendly policies.” The measure is aimed at workers who “have completed one year of continuous service”. “By improving the employee care system, the company aims to contribute to the sustainable development of Macau,” said Wynn. With this decision, the operator wants to ensure that “its workers have enough time to recover physically and mentally at this important stage”. Remember that Wynn has already been granting seven days of paid leave to parents of newborns since 2018. Melco also explained that the measure, aimed at employees with at least one year of service, demonstrates the company's desire "to foster a caring and supportive work environment. By extending these benefits, the company aims to reinforce its friendly and family-friendly workplace measures." Workers “are our most important asset”, said Lawrence Ho, president of the gaming operator, quoted in a statement. Galaxy, whose licenses are also intended for employees with at least one year of service, said that, over the years, it has remained committed to promoting a work environment favorable to families, setting an example by creating areas with comfortable facilities for breastfeeding in its enterprises. The operator “will continue to review and improve workers’ benefits in line with Macau’s social development needs”, he guaranteed. For MGM, the initiative demonstrates the concessionaire's “full support” for the Government's fertility incentive initiatives, “while further strengthening the culture of family and belonging”. Wendy Yu, vice-president of Human Resources at MGM, said that “the increase in maternity and paternity leaves demonstrates the commitment to the well-being of workers and their contributions to creating a birth-friendly environment in Macau”. "Creating a friendly and caring experience has always been at the core of our company culture. We firmly believe that our members' family happiness is a vital foundation for individual well-being," said Wendy Yu.
P.M.

item.title: Lecture about Camilo Pessanha resulted in a “movement” to create a Museum
item.description:
The life and work of Camilo Pessanha deserve Macau to honor him with the creation of a museum, where some of his most famous poems can be displayed to the public. This was the challenge launched, with a request for public subscription, by several speakers at a lecture, which took place yesterday at Casa Garden. They consider this to be a way of recognizing the importance of the writer for the territory. Camilo Pessanha's poetry and the literary current as a whole were the central themes of the colloquium
VÍTOR REBELO
Full room at Casa Garden to watch the lecture on the life and work of Camilo Pessanha, in a pre-event of the official program of this year's edition of the Rota das Letras Literary Festival. In yesterday's session, held on the exact day, March 1st, which marked 100 years since the death of the poet and writer, who lived in Macau for the last 32 years of his life, several speakers participated in the round table, namely António Carlos Cortez, poet, professor and essayist, the writers and journalists Carlos Morais José and Christopher Chu, the professors from the University of Macau (UM) Diego Giménez and Maggie Hoi and the architect Carlos Marreiros. The guidance was provided by Sérgio Guimarães de Sousa, a professor at UM. For around an hour and a half, we talked mainly about the work of Camilo Pessanha and his book “Clepsidra”, but we also discussed the writer's biography. In the final part of the conversation, the need to create a Museum was discussed again, which had already been raised in recent years by some historians and scholars of the poet's work in recent years. Following the colloquium, a “movement” and a “request” emerged to Portuguese residents in Macau to launch a public subscription for this purpose. António Carlos Cortez, who develops scientific activity in the area of ​​Portuguese Literature and Culture as a researcher at the University of Minho and who has dedicated himself to analyzing the writer's work, says “it makes sense” that Macau has a museum that essentially describes Camilo Pessanha's literary legacy. “We often talk about a poet, but not about his work, so it would be essential for this to happen through the creation of a museum”, said the professor and essayist, adding that “it would be fundamental” to achieve this objective, in this specific case of Camilo Pessanha. “It’s not exactly just life, the episodes”, he emphasized. “If you build the museum, very well, life is still important, but then try talking about the work, placing verses from Camilo Pessanha's poems around the space and, why not also, placing verses in the streets of the city center”, observed the literary critic, who recited, with some emotion, some passages from Pessanha's poems in “Clepsidra”. Journalist Carlos Morais José stated that since 2012 he has been fighting for the Museum. “At this moment, regardless of the poetry itself, what is important is the figure of Camilo Pessanha in Macau and what he represents for the city and the Portuguese community, because I think there is a duty for this community to celebrate, commemorate and remember one of its most honest representatives”. Namely, he points out, “he owes him a museum that documents his life and work”. In his opinion, this space “should be an initiative of the Portuguese community and its institutions, some of which Pessanha belonged to”. Supporting the “movement” is also Rita Santos, present in the room alongside deputy José Pereira Coutinho. The counselor of the Portuguese Communities of the China Circle praised the initiative and recognized the need for this museum, hoping that the subscription will be a reality and that it can move forward as quickly as possible. On this afternoon of tribute to Camilo Pessanha, the session moderator launched the challenge to the guests, all of whom had in-depth knowledge of the poet's work. António Carlos Cortez opened the period of analysis, starting by talking about poetry in a general, non-personalized way, and later directing his focus to the honored Camilo Pessanha. “In relation to poetry as a whole, it is fundamental, because it continues to ask each reader to participate”, he stated, adding that “when we read a poem, its language, be it more metaphorical, or less metaphorical, be it more descriptive or less descriptive, the truth is that a poem, by definition, puts the reader before a terrible question, which is the proposition of an enigma”. Now, he reinforced, “as an enigma, as a mysterious language, as a posing of problems, first and foremost the problem of deciphering metaphors, that poetry seems to me to make a lot of sense to be read and to be taught in a time of digital triumphalism”. The teacher said that “increasingly, and we see this with great concern, in short, in several instances, we realize that, from teachers, many students, a little in civil society, in journalism, the issue of language is the terrible issue because impoverishment is real, accelerated and profound”. In this context, he stressed that “poetry brings us to this world that continues to be a human world, which is the world of the proposition of the enigma and, on the other hand, poetry is one of the expressions of the human faculty of language and the faculty of imagination”. As he highlighted, nowadays, “in the face of an absolutely violent world, in the face of a world in which everything seems to be reduced to the mere immediate exchange of should and what should be, poetry, in short, as an expression of the human, is an enigma, it is, for a participative reader, the proposition of the enigma and it asks for our imagination”. When we read a poem, he asserted, “we will also activate the faculty of imagination, and Pessanha's poetry is special”. Meanwhile, Carlos Marreiros, author of the statue of the poet found in Jardim das Artes, and who has been illustrating and drawing Camilo Pessanha since the 1970s, said that he does so “because his poems are like sculpting the sound in words”. About the life of the writer who died 100 years ago, the architect and artist told some stories, not forgetting to talk about the “hypocrisy” of some personalities at that time in Macau, “who criticized the poet’s stance, but went to his house to be consulted and ask for advice”.
Poetry “justifies” Portuguese presence in Macau For his part, Carlos Morais José said it is interesting that Macau has this deep connection to Portuguese poetry, giving the example of Camões, “because, if we think about it, it is not Fortaleza do Monte, it is not the Government Palace, it is poetry that founds and justifies our presence here in Macau”. The journalist maintained that Camilo Pessanha is the “maximum example of this”, justifying that the poet came to the most remote point of the Portuguese presence in the world. He also highlighted that Camilo Pessanha was one of the few Portuguese people who, upon arriving in Macau, immediately hired a Chinese teacher and after a very short time he knew how to speak Cantonese. “This is something very rare”, he stressed, adding that “there was an immersion of Camilo in Chinese culture which I think has to do with the desire for erasure, with the desire for dissolution, even if these are my speculations”. The round table was also attended by Christopher Chu, co-author of “Camilo Pessanha’s Macau Stories” with Maggie Hoi. He highlighted Camilo Pessanha as a fundamental witness to Portugal's transition from monarchy to republic and a historical landmark in Macau.” His book aims to introduce the poet and the history of Macau to non-Portuguese-speaking audiences, portraying him as a judge, teacher and intellectual passionate about Chinese art. Chu showed photographs from the time when the poet lived in Macau, essentially addressing his lifestyle, which despite opium and not having widespread acceptance among some sectors of society, held meetings with various personalities, including Sun Yat Sen. Maggie Choi said, for her part, that many ethnic Chinese people talk about Camilo Pessanha, “but a large number know little about the poet’s work”. In turn, Diego Gimenez, who works in digital humanities, spoke of the importance of digitizing Camilo Pessanha's poems and specifically his book “Clepsidra”, noting that technological development requires it. “I believe that we must actively participate in the production and preservation of literary heritage in a digital context”, he mentioned.

item.title: Iran defies Trump with promise to avenge Khamenei's death
item.description:
epa12786203 Mourners react following the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; at Enqelab Square in Tehran, Iran, 01 March 2026. According to a statement from Iranian state media issued on 01 March 2026, Khamenei was killed in an airstrike during a joint United States (US) and Israeli military campaign that began on 28 February 2026. EPA/ABEDIN TAHERKENAREH
Iran promised yesterday to avenge the death of its supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, killed in Israeli and US airstrikes, in defiance of US President Donald Trump, who threatened unprecedented attacks if retaliation continues. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian declared that revenge is a “duty and a legitimate right”, while Grand Ayatollah Nasser Makarem Shirazi, a prominent figure in Iranian Shiism, called for jihad against the US and Israel.
epa12784404 Participants light torches in their smartphones as they stand in front of a small stage at the Brandenburg Gate during a rally of exile Iranians as they pass by the US embassy in Berlin, Germany, 28 February 2026. The rally took place under the motto ‘Freedom for Iran. Protest against false information from ARD and ZDF’ following joint Israel-US military strikes targeting multiple locations in Iran in the early hours of 28 February 2026. EPA/CLEMENS BILAN
Ali Larijani, head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, predicted “an unforgettable lesson for international oppressors”. "Yesterday, Iran fired missiles at the US and Israel, causing damage. Today, we will attack them with a force they have never known," he wrote in capital letters on social media, in Trump style. Iranian authorities announced 40 days of mourning for the death of Khamenei, whose daughter, son-in-law and granddaughter also died in the attack, according to the country's media. The Israeli army declared that it had eliminated 40 high-ranking officers. The Iranian state press also confirmed the deaths of the head of the Revolutionary Guard, Mohammad Pakpour; the Chief of Staff, Abdolrahim Mousavi; the Minister of Defense, Aziz Nasirzadeh; and of Ali Shamkhani, a close advisor to the Supreme Leader and head of the National Defense Council. The Iranian Red Crescent reported at least 201 dead and hundreds injured. The Iranian judiciary stated that an attack on a school in the south of the country, on Saturday, killed 108 people, but this report requires independent confirmation and the Israeli army assured that it had “no knowledge” of this attack. Iranian state television confirmed Khamenei's death in the early hours of Sunday, after Trump announced the elimination of the 86-year-old cleric, a declared enemy of the West who he described as “one of the most evil people in history”. The announcement of his death was greeted with applause by some Iranians, but as soon as it was confirmed, pro-government demonstrations broke out. “Death to America!” they shouted. The Iranian military announced attacks on 27 US bases in the Persian Gulf and Iraqi Kurdistan, a day after launching a series of missiles against Israel, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Bahrain, killing at least two people in Abu Dhabi and one in Tel Aviv. Trump warned he would strike with “unprecedented force” if the retaliations continued and again called on the Iranians to rise up and take power. The Islamic Republic has been on the brink of collapse since the brutal repression of anti-government protests in December and January, which left thousands dead. Washington also accuses Tehran of refusing to give in in negotiations on an agreement on its nuclear and ballistic missile programs. Both Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Trump stated that the operation will last “as long as necessary”. This Sunday air raid sirens sounded and several explosions were heard in Tel Aviv. According to international agencies, the scenario was repeated in the north and center of Tehran. Iran's first retaliatory attacks on Saturday hit all Gulf states except Oman, which mediated negotiations between the US and Iran. But yesterday, the Omani port of Duqm was hit by two drones, according to the local news agency, and an oil tanker off the coast of the sultanate suffered the same fate.
Triumvirate assumes transition Iran is now in the hands of a triumvirate in charge of the transition. It is composed of President Pezeshkian; by the head of the judiciary, Gholamhosein Mohseni Ejei; and by Alireza Arafi, religious dignitary and member of the Assembly of Experts and the Council of the Revolutionary Guard, as announced by the government. Reza Pahlavi, son of the late Shah, a pro-Western leader ousted by the Islamic Revolution in 1979, believes that any successor from within the system is illegitimate. Furthermore, he predicts that, with Khamenei's death, “the Islamic Republic has effectively come to an end and will soon be relegated to the dustbin of history”. Pahlavi, who spent most of his life in exile near Washington, has presented himself as a transitional figure towards a secular democracy, but does not have the support of the entire opposition. The US and Israeli attacks against Iran provoked strong international reactions, which oscillate between support, including from Iranian exiles abroad, and disapproval, with great concern about the risk of regional escalation. In Iraq and Pakistan, mobs attempted to invade American diplomatic missions. In the Pakistani city of Karachi, at least eight people were killed during pro-Iran protests, according to Muhammad Amin, spokesman for the Edhi Foundation rescue service. The attacks also caused the biggest disruption to global air traffic since the Covid-19 pandemic, with thousands of flights to the Middle East delayed or cancelled.
China condemns Iranian leader's death China yesterday firmly condemned the death of the Iranian supreme leader and reaffirmed its call for an “immediate halt to military actions”. The death of Ali Khamenei constitutes “a serious violation of Iran's sovereignty and security”, as well as “a violation of the objectives and principles of the Charter of the United Nations and the fundamental norms of international relations”, said the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a statement.
JTM with international agencies

item.title: UM received 40 universities to strengthen cooperation
item.description:
International cooperation in higher education, joint academic development and student and teacher exchanges were topics covered on the University of Macau's International Partners' Day. Four dozen institutions from around the world participated in the event
The University of Macau (UM) held International Partners Day, which was attended by more than 50 representatives from almost 40 higher education institutions from around the world, including the universities of Oxford (United Kingdom), KU Leuven (Belgium), Barcelona (Spain), Macquarie (Australia) and Twente (Netherlands). The aim of the event was to further expand UM's international cooperation, focusing on facilitating international educational exchange. According to a statement from UM, the “International Partner Day” not only highlighted “UM’s growing influence and appeal in the global higher education landscape”, but also “opened new avenues for cooperation with renowned universities around the world through in-depth exchanges”. The event, the text adds, “further strengthened UM’s integration into the global higher education network and contributed to the development of the Hengqin Deep Cooperation Zone as an international innovation and technology center with global influence.” During the meeting, delegations participated in discussions on topics such as international cooperation in higher education, joint academic development and student and teacher exchanges. The meeting with universities from Europe, Asia and the Americas took place at the UM Guest House and vice-rector Michael Hui presented a detailed overview of UM's development, research achievements and internationalization strategy, as well as sharing the latest practices and initiatives in talent training, academic research and inter-university collaboration. The person in charge also learned about the experiences of participating universities and ideas for cooperation, “thus promoting UM’s strategic partnerships with universities around the world”. Meanwhile, Wang Chunming, director of the UM Office of Global Affairs, explained the development and culture of the UM campus at the University Gallery. Representatives also visited the College of Business Administration and Moon Chun Memorial College, gaining insight into the university's educational model and areas of excellence.
V.R.

item.title: Green Week offers more than 40 activities
item.description:
Green Week, which takes place between the 7th and 15th of March, will offer more than 40 activities to citizens, including the maintenance of tree seedlings in the Taipa Grande Natural Park, guided tours, games and workshops. As usual, there will also be distribution of plants in different areas of the city
The 45th edition of Macau's Green Week, which will take place between the 7th and 15th of March, will provide more than 40 activities “getting closer to Nature” aimed at schools, associations and all residents, indicated the Institute for Municipal Affairs (IAM), which organizes the initiative. This edition continues to have as its theme the “Transmission of love for afforestation for the construction of a beautiful city”. During the event, several guided ecological tours and experience activities will be held, “to raise awareness about green buildings, ecological resources and the current conservation situation in Macau”. On that occasion, together with the population, “tree seedlings will be maintained in the Taipa Grande Natural Park, to protect natural ecological resources”, informed the IAM. This activity, entitled “March of the 45th edition of Macau Green Week and Maintenance of Planted Trees”, takes place on the 15th. In addition, the organization will continue to organize the ecological visit to Hengqin, “with a view to providing more opportunities for the public to get closer to Nature”, can be read in the press release. The inauguration of Green Week takes place at Seac Pai Van Park, on March 7th, at 3pm, with activities continuing the following day, from 2pm to 6pm. At the site, there will be themed tents that present information about wetlands, ancient trees of recognized value, work related to the conservation of butterflies and otters, techniques for planting and maintaining domestic bonsai trees, taxidermy, insect hotels, ecological recovery and living with low carbon emissions, explained the IAM. Tents will also be available with games and workshops to popularize science, with the aim of “providing information on afforestation and conservation at various levels to citizens and raising awareness of the good treatment of Nature”. As part of the more than 40 activities to be carried out during Green Week, the IAM highlights the new thematic activity “Getting to know the ecology of Seac Pai Van”, which includes a guided tour of the new Educational Pavilion on Amphibians and Reptiles, the Bird Observation Garden and the Butterfly Garden. This edition will be held in conjunction with Arbor Day in China, and on March 12th, at Largo do Senado, the activity “Philatelic Stamp Commemorating Arbor Day in China” will take place. Also during Green Week, IAM will organize six sessions of plant distribution activities in different areas of Macau, under the theme “Green City”, with citizens being able to take plants home. At the same time, fees for the Guia Cable Car and children's quadricycles on the Hac Sá off-road vehicle circuit will be waived. This edition is co-organized with several associations and institutions.
CP.

item.title: Recycled water already circulates in Seac Pai Van and UM
item.description:
default
Since yesterday, recycled water has been circulating in public housing in Seac Pai Van and the University of Macau, said DSAMA, recalling that this type of water will be used to flush toilet tanks and irrigate green spaces. The recycled water tariff corresponds to 85% of the drinking water tariff
The Coloane Recycled Water Station phase I has already started supplying recycled water to public housing in Seac Pai Van and the University of Macau (UM), for flushing toilet tanks and irrigating green spaces, said the Maritime and Water Affairs Bureau (DSAMA). Specifically, Seac Pai Van includes the Koi Nga, Lok Kuan and Ip Heng buildings, as well as the Seac Pai Van Official School, the Center for Language Pedagogical Activities, the Center for Technical-Vocational Education Activities, the Community Complex, as well as the Social and Health Services Building. The scope of supply to UM includes the Operational Station of the Fire Department of Hengqin Island and the CPSP Station of the New Campus of the University of Macau. According to DSAMA, users can use recycled water without needing to comply with other formalities. The recycled water tariff, remember, corresponds to 85% of the drinking water tariff, and the invoice issued by Sociedade de Abastecimento de Águas de Macau, S.A. will detail the volume and tariff of drinking water and recycled water. Therefore, users will pay the total amount shown on the invoice as usual, the organization clarified. DSAMA further explained that recycled water is colorless and odorless, being subjected to daily testing, with the results being published every day. At the end of February, DSAMA carried out several activities related to recycled water. The last one, at the Official School of Seac Pai Van, was attended by more than 500 residents, including children and the elderly. “Through this event, residents’ understanding and acceptance of recycled water was increased, as well as awareness of saving and valuing water”, noted the organization.

item.title: Commercial and industrial sectors urged to “guide” young people
item.description:
The commercial and industrial sectors are responsible for the “achievements” achieved in the development of the economy, according to the Chief Executive. Speaking at the spring dinner of the Macau Commercial Association, Sam Hou Fai expressed five expectations for 2026, hoping that the two sectors will seize the opportunities arising from the measures and policies, be “pioneers” in the construction of the Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, “builders” of social harmony and “guides” for the young
VÍTOR REBELO
The Chief Executive said at the spring dinner of the Macau Commercial Association that the commercial and industrial sectors contributed to the economy and society experiencing a stable recovery and continuous development last year. “With the acceleration of economic diversification, new advances were achieved in the development of the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin and the integration between Macau and Hengqin was strengthened”, highlighted Sam Hou Fai, adding that “these achievements are due, above all, to the active support and joint effort of all sectors, particularly members of the industrial and commercial sector”. Indicating that the Government will work with all sectors of society to “effectively implement the development initiatives of the next phase”, the official presented five expectations to the Macau Commercial Association and the industrial and business sector for this year 2026. First, it hopes that the commercial and industrial sectors will continue to “uphold the fundamental values ​​of patriotism and love for Macau”, contributing with “renewed and greater strength” to the stable and lasting implementation of the cause of the “one country, two systems” principle. The Chief Executive also asked them to be “drivers” of adequate diversification of the economy, promote the “qualitative development” of diversified “1+4” industries through participation in the planning of national development strategies and the 3rd Five-Year Plan of the Macao SAR, and continue to play “their role as a bridge, a link, a connection”, in order to create “more platforms for the promotion of Macau” as “a center, a platform, a base” and “a pole”. Furthermore, he urged leaders from the commercial and industrial sectors to be “pioneers” in the construction of the Hengqin Cooperation Zone, increase investment for the integrated development of Macau and Hengqin and mobilize quality resources for both regions. On the other hand, Sam Hou Fai wants the commercial and industrial sectors to be “builders” of social harmony, defending “the spirit of mutual support and collaborative negotiation, always taking into account the global situation”, and safeguarding “harmonious and stable” labor relations. Finally, he called on the association and both sectors to be “guides” for youth development, creating “opportunities for the younger generation to grow and develop, expanding their horizons and competitiveness through various channels”. It also hopes that they will promote an “innovative and entrepreneurial environment that encourages the courage to renew, experiment and take initiatives, to support young people in realizing their ambitions and achieving success on the broad stages of Macau, Hengqin and the Greater Bay Area”.

item.title: Macao SAR and Guangxi strengthen cooperation and synergies
item.description: The Chief Executive stressed that the Macao SAR is “a crucial bridge for the promotion of Guangxi products in Portuguese-speaking countries”, according to a statement from the Social Communication Office. In a meeting with Chen Gang, chairman of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress of Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region, Sam Hou Fai promised to “continue to strengthen synergistic development and pragmatic cooperation with Guangxi”. During the meeting, which took place at the Government Headquarters, the leader of the Macao SAR stated that “Macau is not just an important partner for Guangxi to explore the foreign market”. He praised the “close connection” between the two regions, whose collaboration “has been expanding uninterruptedly”, in areas such as “the economy and commerce, the well-being of the population, infrastructures, cutting-edge technology, humanistic and cultural exchange”. In gratitude for the “attention and support provided by Guangxi” for the development of the Macao SAR, the Chief highlighted the “enormous contribution to the construction of the Datengxia Hydraulic Complex, which guaranteed the “safe and stable” supply of water to Macau and the Pearl River Delta region, constituting a demonstration of “significant results of regional synergistic development”. Sam Hou Fai also said he wanted to “jointly expand the markets of ASEAN countries and Portuguese-speaking countries, with the aim of promoting high-quality construction in the Greater Bay Area”, it reads. Speaking to Radio Macau, the Secretary for Social Affairs and Culture, O Lam, defended the creation of a cooperation system that enhances the ecological and well-being resources of the autonomous region of southern China and the international advantages of the MSAR in the tourism sector. Lam also proposed the development of wellness tourist routes between the two regions and a commercial relationship between Guangxi and Portuguese-speaking countries mediated by the Macao SAR. The Secretary also suggested the joint exploration of intelligent models of care for the elderly and a joint training and attraction system, in the areas of health and cultural and tourist management.

item.title: “Girlfriends” marked Tracy Choi’s return to Macau cinemas
item.description:
On Friday, MGM Macau’s “Fantasy Box” hosted the Macau premiere of Tracy Choi’s latest film, “Girlfriends”. The film features a renowned cast, including actresses from Macau, Hong Kong and Taiwan, and chronicles the growth of a young woman through three distinct phases of her life.
Tracy Choi premiered her latest film, “Girlfriends”, in Macau on Friday. The feature film, a co-production between Macao SAR, Hong Kong and Taiwan, portrays the growth of a young woman, through three romantic relationships, in three cities and over the course of thirty years. For the occasion, the public filled the “Fantasy Box” at MGM Macau, on a night that the event promoters described as “another milestone for Macau cinema on the international stage”. The film traces the protagonist's personal journey through three different stages of her life, namely at 17 years old, when she is still “naive and inexperienced”, at 22, a period in which she appears “hesitant and without courage”, and at 34, when she has already “matured with the challenges of life”. The character's growth reveals that “although love can be painful, it is always worth fighting for”, can be read in the press release. The feature film attracted international attention, after its world premiere at the Busan International Film Festival, and having accumulated three nominations for the 44th Hong Kong Film Awards, including for Best Supporting Actress and Best Newcomer, both for Elizabeth Tang, and for Best Original Film Music, with “Ordinary People”, written and performed by Panther Chan Lei. In fact, Tang's performance also earned her a nomination for the 62nd Horse Awards Golden. For this film, the acclaimed Macau director featured a renowned cast that included, in addition to Elizabeth Tang, local actress Eliz Lao, Fish Liew, nominated for Best Actress at the 44th Hong Kong Film Awards, Jennifer Yu, Best Actress at the 42nd Hong Kong Film Awards, Natalie Hsu, winner of the 2025 “Asian Star Award” at the New York Asian Film Festival, and Han Ning, artist from Taiwan. The night at “Fantasy Box” began with an opening toast and continued with the screening of the film, followed by a question session with the audience, during which the director and the six actresses shared stories and highlights of the production. Nominated for the Hong Kong Film Awards in 2017, with the project “Sisterhood”, Tracy Choi has continued to dedicate herself to the film industry over the years. Reflecting on the premiere, the director admitted that she shares “the same passion for cinema and entertainment as MGM”. "Bringing the film home to Macau is both exciting and stressful. Sharing it with so many family and friends was truly moving, and we deeply felt the support of the entire Macau community. 10 years ago, I worked with lead actresses Fish Liew and Jennifer Yu on 'Sisterhood,' which was also filmed in Macau. Reuniting with them for 'Girlfriends' makes this city even more meaningful to us," shared Tracy Choi.
P.M.

item.title: Rain did not “stop” the Horse Parade
item.description:
The rain did not stop many people from gathering along the route, in the north of the city, to watch the Year of the Horse Celebration Parade pass by, indicated the Directorate of Tourism Services. The festivities continue tomorrow with the third fireworks and drone show
Despite the rain that was felt over the weekend, the Year of the Horse Celebration Parade took place on Saturday, livening up the northern part of the city. A total of 17 floats “brought happiness”, celebrating the New Year with residents and visitors, “continuing the lively and festive atmosphere of the Lunar New Year”, highlighted the Directorate of Tourism Services (DST). “Even on this rainy day, many stopped to watch the parade, attracting crowds along the way”, noted the organization led by Helena de Senna Fernandes. The parade began at Rua Norte do Patane, passing through Avenida do Conselheiro Borja, Estrada do Arco, Estrada da Areia Preta, Avenida de Venceslau de Morais, Rua Quatro do Bairro da Areia Preta, Avenida da Longevidade, and then continuing to Rua do Mercado de Iao Hon, ending at Jardim do Mercado de Iao Hon. The final cultural and artistic show of the Parade was held precisely at Jardim do Mercado Iao Hon. The program was presented by Sands, SJM Resorts, Wynn Macau, Galaxy Entertainment Group, Prudential Insurance Company Limited (Macau Branch) and Melco Crown Entertainment, and also included the participation of Andrew Chan, winifai, Kane Ao Ieong, among other artists from Hong Kong and Macau. The cars that participated in the Parade will now be on display in Praça do Tap Seac, until March 8. The light installations on the floats are switched on daily between 5pm and 10pm. The Year of the Horse Celebration Parade program includes raffles and games celebrating Chinese New Year. Until tomorrow, in the interactive area of ​​DST's Wechat mini-program, two small games alluding to the Chinese New Year will be launched: “Immediate Happiness” and “Galloping Horses”. Participants who complete the interactive games will have the opportunity to participate in a draw and win prizes, said DST. On the other hand, remember that the third drone and fireworks show will take place on the 15th day of the Lunar New Year (3 March), at 9pm, on the riverside in front of the Macau Tower. Coinciding with the Lantern Festival, the show will consist of a display of around 15 minutes with 3,888 drones, which includes drawings of elements related to “Chinese Valentine's Day”, followed by a 10-minute fireworks show, with the entire display having a total duration of around 25 minutes. To watch the show, DST suggests six points: Anim ‘Arte NAM VAN, Avenida Dr. Sun Yat-Sen between the Kun Iam Ecumenical Center and the Kun Iam Statue Marginal Leisure Area, Avenida de Sagres (next to the Hotel Mandarin Oriental), the promenade along the waterfront of the Science Center, the Caminho Marginal do Lago (next to the Hotel YOHO Ilha de Tesouro) and Avenida do Oceano da Taipa.

item.title: Gulbenkian Choir will carry out cultural tour of Macau
item.description: The Gulbenkian Choir from Portugal will give three performances at this year's “Hong Kong Arts Festival” (HKAF) and the delegation will travel to the territory for a cultural tour next Friday. According to the Instituto Português do Oriente (IPOR), the visit aims to provide the group with “a deeper understanding of Sino-Portuguese culture, cuisine and the development of sacred music”. During the first stop, participants can choose to obtain information about Macanese-Portuguese gastronomy from the famous chef Antonieta Manhão (Neta), or consult the HKAF travel guide to explore places such as Bairro de São Lázaro and Igreja de São Lázaro. The second stop will be led by the diocesan director of Sacred Music of the Diocese of Macau, Andrew Leung, who will guide a trip to the Cathedral of the Nativity of Our Lady, the Seminary of Saint Joseph and the Diocesan Historical and Heritage Archive, “discovering the beauty of the organs installed in the two churches”, highlights the IPOR statement. To complete this “culturally enriching” experience, carried out by those responsible for HKAF, the members of the Gulbenkian Choir will also enjoy Portuguese sacred music in the Church of the Seminário de São José, before organizing their own dinner.

item.title: Antonio Tejero, protagonist of the failed coup in Spain in 1981, has died
item.description:
Former civil guard Antonio Tejero de Molina, who in 1981 kidnapped the Government and Spanish deputies inside Parliament for 17 hours, in a failed coup attempt, died last week, aged 93. On February 23, 1981, Antonio Tejero led an attempted coup d'état filmed by Spanish public television (RTVE) cameras installed inside the plenary room of the Spanish Parliament. The image of the then civil guard entering the Congress of Deputies (Lower House of Parliament/Cortes) with a pistol in hand that day remained in the history of Spain and 23-F (as the failed coup d'état attempt of 1981 is known) is one of the episodes considered most striking and decisive of the so-called “Spanish transition” from the Francoist dictatorship (led by General Francisco Franco, who died in 1975) to democracy. In fact, with the coup attempt, Tejero tried to get King Juan Carlos I to support him. Many hours later, he saw his plan failed. The King supported the democratic transition that continues today. After the coup attempt, Tejero was tried and convicted of military rebellion, along with two former generals Alfonso Armada and Jaime Milans del Bosch. Tejero was sentenced to 30 years in prison and was released from prison, with parole, in 1996, after serving half of his sentence. It last appeared in public in 2019, on the day that Franco's body was removed from the basilica of the Valley of the Fallen (a place on the outskirts of Madrid that is one of the greatest symbols of the Spanish dictatorship) and taken to a cemetery in the town of El Pardo. Tejero joined those nostalgic for the dictatorship that day in protest against the removal of Franco's body from the Valley of the Fallen near the entrance to the cemetery. In more detail, about the attempt, on February 23, 1981, Tejero, who led around 200 civil guards who accompanied him, interrupted the parliamentary session during which Leopoldo Calvo-Sotelo should have been appointed as the new leader of the Government of Spain, following the resignation of then Prime Minister Adoldo Suárez. History has left, among other moments, the cry “Everyone quiet!”, which Tejero launched when he ascended the parliament rostrum after pushing aside the Minister of Defense and hierarchical superior, General Manuel Gutierrez Mellado, who faced him and tried to stop him. This was followed by 17 hours of kidnapping within the parliament of 350 deputies and all members of the Government, as well as employees and journalists, among others, until the surrender of Tejero and his men, around midday on 24 February. At those times, the civil guards even fired shots into the ceiling and asked everyone present to lie down on the floor, an order that three men did not comply with: Adolfo Suárez, Gutierrez Mellado and the then leader of the Spanish Communist Party, Santiago Carrillo, who remained seated in their respective places in the plenary, this being another of the images of that day that remained in the recent history of Spain, due to the courage they demonstrated. The short speech that the then King of Spain Juan Carlos I made on television during the early hours of the morning, in which he defended the constitutional order and democracy, was considered decisive for the failure of the coup and the surrender of Tejero. 23-F transformed Juan Carlos I into one of the heroes of the Spanish transition and guarantor of democracy in Spain, although in recent years his true role in this episode has been questioned by several protagonists, including Tejero himself, who said that he was used by Alfonso Armada, the King's former tutor and head of the Royal House. Alfonso Armada intended to head a unity government that emerged from the coup d'état, something that, in Tejero's version, the general had said was agreed with Juan Carlos I. Juan Carlos I, who abdicated in 2014, publicly gave his own version of 23-F for the first time in the memoir he published last year, where he claims to have been betrayed by Alfonso Armada. "Alfonso Armada was by my side for 17 years. I really liked him and he betrayed me. He convinced the generals that he was speaking in my name", he reported. What Spanish justice proved was that the coup had been orchestrated by the two generals and by Tejero, with Alfonso Armada always denying that he was the mastermind of the operation. “The Crown, a symbol of the permanence and unity of the country, cannot tolerate in any way the actions or attitudes of people who seek to forcibly interrupt the democratic process”, stated Juan Carlos in the message broadcast on television at the time. From that moment on, it became clear that the coup would be a failure and the coup plotters surrendered hours later. Before 23-F, Tejero had already been convicted of participating in 1978 in “Operation Galaxy”, a conspiracy that also intended to carry out a coup d'état with an assault on the Moncloa Palace, the headquarters of the Spanish Government.
JTM with Lusa
